name: Triptych_Mech

infobox.Systems: Tri-Rocket(Ranged)EffectDamage and push 3 tiles in a line.(1 damage)Upgrade I+1 Damage ()Deals 1 additional damage to all targets.Upgrade IIBuilding Immune ()This attack will no longer damage Grid Buildings.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theCataclysmsquad.
sections.Strategy:
Due to Tri-Rocket's multi-target damage, the Triptych mech benefits from a pilot that grants Boost, such asKai Miller,Morgan Lejeuneor another pilot with a Boostskill.
The starting weapons 3-tile range lets the Triptych Mech target the tile directly in front of its attack range and push away webbing melee Vek if it needs to. In its Cataclysm squad, the rockets can be used to crack tiles while pushing an enemy into the created chasm. Since the rockets are fired in a straight line, the movement upgrade to  the Mech and a pilot skill that offers more movement are helpful to let the Tri-Rocket weapon cover as many columns as possible.
The multi-targeting also adds a lot of bump damage in tight corners (though beware of pushing enemies into buildings). The+1 Damageupgrade — while expensive at 3 reactor cores — is the priority to get for the weapon, as it will allow its damage to scale well on the multiple targets it hits.Buildings Immuneis more niche compared to other Ranged weapons with wider area targetting, although it can still be helpful for letting the Triptych fire at enemies wedged in tight corners with buildings. However, it is better to prioritize the other Mech weapons in Cataclysm, as their upgrades offer more value for the squad to create more chasms.
It is worth considering the upgrade to the Triptych Mechs HP before taking on the Volcanic Hive, given its low starting health can make it easy prey for thePsion Tyrant. Also consider another weapon that can let the Triptych Mech fire in alternate angles than what Tri-Rocket can handle; as an example, the rangedMicro-Artilleryweapon naturally provides three hits on the opposite tiles once upgraded, and thus naturally complements Tri-Rockets range.
sections.Trivia: Atriptychis an artwork divided into three pieces or sections.

Strategy
[
]
Due to Tri-Rocket's multi-target damage, the Triptych mech benefits from a pilot that grants Boost, such as
Kai Miller
,
Morgan Lejeune
or another pilot with a Boost
skill
.
The starting weapons 3-tile range lets the Triptych Mech target the tile directly in front of its attack range and push away webbing melee Vek if it needs to. In its Cataclysm squad, the rockets can be used to crack tiles while pushing an enemy into the created chasm. Since the rockets are fired in a straight line, the movement upgrade to  the Mech and a pilot skill that offers more movement are helpful to let the Tri-Rocket weapon cover as many columns as possible.
The multi-targeting also adds a lot of bump damage in tight corners (though beware of pushing enemies into buildings). The
+1 Damage
upgrade — while expensive at 3 reactor cores — is the priority to get for the weapon, as it will allow its damage to scale well on the multiple targets it hits.
Buildings Immune
is more niche compared to other Ranged weapons with wider area targetting, although it can still be helpful for letting the Triptych fire at enemies wedged in tight corners with buildings. However, it is better to prioritize the other Mech weapons in Cataclysm, as their upgrades offer more value for the squad to create more chasms.
It is worth considering the upgrade to the Triptych Mechs HP before taking on the Volcanic Hive, given its low starting health can make it easy prey for the
Psion Tyrant
. Also consider another weapon that can let the Triptych Mech fire in alternate angles than what Tri-Rocket can handle; as an example, the ranged
Micro-Artillery
weapon naturally provides three hits on the opposite tiles once upgraded, and thus naturally complements Tri-Rockets range.
